LATEST
Canvas Down, ShinyHunters Threatens Schools' Data Leak Amidst Incompetence | The vaunted educational platform Canvas, owned by Instructure, experienced a significant outage after hackers, ShinyHunters, claimed responsibility for a data breach exposing student names, emails, IDs, and messages. The hackers openly mocked Instructure's prior attempts at security, offering a settlement to prevent data release by a specific deadline, showcasing a predictable pattern of corporate negligence and the inevitable exploitation of vulnerabilities. •
Japan's Foreign Minister Motegi Visits Kenya: A Statement on 'Friendly Relations' and Global Ambitions | Japan's Foreign Minister delivers a speech on bilateral relations with Kenya, highlighting cooperation in various fields while subtly weaving in geopolitical interests and economic strategies. •
Kenyan Cheetahs Shipped Off to Gujarat's Banni Grasslands: Another Wild Goose Chase? | Gujarat thinks it's getting a wildlife trophy. More likely, it's getting a costly headache and another failed conservation experiment. •
Rachel Zoe's Brand Rebuild: A Divorce-Fueled Rebranding Effort for Financial Survival | Rachel Zoe, once a fixture of reality television and celebrity styling, is now navigating a post-divorce brand resurrection. This endeavor, heavily reliant on a strategic return to Bravo and a significant investment deal, underscores the precarious nature of personal brands and the relentless pursuit of commercial viability in a saturated market. The pivot from high-end aspirations to mass-market accessibility highlights the harsh realities of sustaining a business in the modern entertainment and fashion landscape. •
The RBI's Delusional Resilience in a Fragmenting World | The Reserve Bank of India projects a facade of stability while global trade wars and internal corporate scandals threaten to dismantle its fragile economic narrative. •
Canvas Down, ShinyHunters Threatens Schools' Data Leak Amidst Incompetence | The vaunted educational platform Canvas, owned by Instructure, experienced a significant outage after hackers, ShinyHunters, claimed responsibility for a data breach exposing student names, emails, IDs, and messages. The hackers openly mocked Instructure's prior attempts at security, offering a settlement to prevent data release by a specific deadline, showcasing a predictable pattern of corporate negligence and the inevitable exploitation of vulnerabilities. •
Japan's Foreign Minister Motegi Visits Kenya: A Statement on 'Friendly Relations' and Global Ambitions | Japan's Foreign Minister delivers a speech on bilateral relations with Kenya, highlighting cooperation in various fields while subtly weaving in geopolitical interests and economic strategies. •

Rachel Zoe's Brand Rebuild: A Divorce-Fueled Rebranding Effort for Financial Survival | Rachel Zoe, once a fixture of reality television and celebrity styling, is now navigating a post-divorce brand resurrection. This endeavor, heavily reliant on a strategic return to Bravo and a significant investment deal, underscores the precarious nature of personal brands and the relentless pursuit of commercial viability in a saturated market. The pivot from high-end aspirations to mass-market accessibility highlights the harsh realities of sustaining a business in the modern entertainment and fashion landscape. •
The RBI's Delusional Resilience in a Fragmenting World | The Reserve Bank of India projects a facade of stability while global trade wars and internal corporate scandals threaten to dismantle its fragile economic narrative. •

#Censorship

Business
8 Feb 2026

NewsGuard Files Lawsuit Against FTC Over Alleged Censorship

NewsGuard has sued the Federal Trade Commission, alleging that Chairman Andrew Ferguson unlawfully barred an advertising agency from using its news ratings.
Investigation
22 Nov 2025

EXPOSED: How Gava is Using Your Taxes to Pay ‘Keyboard Warriors’ to Silence You (While the Cybercrime Bill Waits to Finish the Job)

A fearless exposé of the Kenyan government’s state-sponsored digital harassment campaign—and how the proposed Cybercrime Bill threatens to turn soft intimidation into legal jail time for dissenters.
